---
title: "README"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-15"
---

# Seeing the Invisible: Spatial Auditing of Energy Poverty in Madrid

This repository contains the source code and replicable pipeline for the **Master's Thesis (TFM)** submitted in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the *Master in Computational Social Science* at Universidad Carlos III de Madrid (UC3M).

**Author:** María Garcés Blázquez  
**Academic Year:** 2025--2026  

---

## Project Overview
The main objective of this study is to estimate, predict, and audit energy poverty at the census section level within the municipality of Madrid. To achieve this, we develop a hybrid 4-phase methodological framework that integrates traditional socioeconomic administrative data with satellite remote sensing variables derived from Google Earth Engine (GEE).

The computational workflow implemented in this project is structured as follows:
1. **Phase 1 — Census Master File Construction & Imputation:** Integration of 10 administrative data sources and treatment of missing values using non-parametric multivariate imputation via `missForest`.
2. **Phase 2 — Small Area Estimation (SAE):** Downscaling regional survey data using a Synthetic Regression Estimator (Rao & Molina, 2015) via a logistic mixed-effects model trained on microdata from the Living Conditions Survey (ECV).
3. **Phase 3 — Predictive Models (Dual Random Forest):** Construction of complementary Random Forest classifiers using environmental satellite metrics (LST, NDVI, NDBI, Albedo) and cadastral infrastructure features to predict energy vulnerability status.
4. **Phase 4 — Discrepancy Analysis & Group Characterization:** Identification and statistical validation of territorial subgroups to isolate cases of "invisible energy poverty" (spatial false negatives and spatial false positives).

---

## Repository Structure

* `tfm-energy-poverty.Rproj`: RStudio project configuration file.
* `README.md`: This markdown documentation file providing replication instructions.
* `code_tfm.qmd`: Centralized Quarto script containing the entire executable pipeline (data processing, missing data CV, SAE modeling, Random Forest training, SHAP values local interpretability, and exploratory visualizations).

### Data Dependencies (`/data`)
Due to GitHub's file size restrictions (strict 100 MB limit per file), the heaviest raw data files have been excluded from this repository using `.gitignore`. 

To fully replicate this pipeline, you must independently download the following files from the Spanish National Statistics Institute (INE) and place them inside your local `data/` folder:
* **`Atlas_umbral_sexo.csv`** (~135 MB): Available at the [INE - Household Income Distribution Atlas](https://www.ine.es/componentes_inebase/ADRH_total_nacional.htm).
* **`SECC_CE_20210101.shp`** (~108 MB): Digital cartography of census sections, available at the [INE - Cartografía Digital](https://www.ine.es/dyngs/DAB/index.htm?cid=1389).

*Note: Smaller supplementary files (`Atlas_renta.csv`, `Atlas_demografia.csv`, `2022.xlsx`, etc.) should remain in your local layout as specified by the paths in the script.*

---

## Requirements & Installation

The entire pipeline has been developed using **R (v4.x)** and compiles into an interactive HTML document via **Quarto**. To install all the required packages to execute the code, run the following command in your R console:

```R
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "sf", "readxl", "mice", "missForest", 
                   "Amelia", "ggplot2", "Hmisc", "patchwork", "lme4", 
                   "MuMIn", "flextable", "scales", "randomForest", 
                   "caret", "pROC", "iml"))
